explicit_text: My grandfather arrived in San Francisco in January 1850 after sailing around the Horn from Kiel. He initially tried his luck in the gold mines but soon returned to San Francisco to run lodging houses and hotels, including the New Orleans Hotel on Post Street. I was born in San Francisco in 1893 and spent my early childhood in the North Beach area, attending school there before moving to Sonoma.
implicit_text: My grandfather arrived in the bustling West Coast city in January 1850 after sailing around the Horn from a northern German port. He initially tried his luck in the gold mines but soon returned to that city to run lodging houses and hotels, including a well-known hotel on Post Street. I was born there in 1893 and spent my early childhood in the North Beach area, attending school there before moving to Sonoma.

explicit_text: Kiel was my grandfather's hometown in Schleswig-Holstein, where his father was a cabinet-maker. He left Kiel in 1849 because he felt oppressed under Danish rule and sought a better future in California. It was from Kiel that he embarked on his journey to San Francisco during the Gold Rush.

explicit_text: My grandfather had a brother who left Kiel at the same time as him but went to South America instead of California. That brother joined the forces of a South American liberator, and unfortunately, they never heard from each other again. So South America was part of my family’s story, though indirectly.

explicit_text: John C. Lutgens was my grandfather’s name. He came from Kiel in 1850 and initially tried mining but soon became a lodging-house keeper in San Francisco by 1852 or 1853. Later he ran the New Orleans Hotel on Post Street and eventually built the Union Hotel in Sonoma. He was also secretary of the Franklin Hospital in San Francisco before he passed away in 1904.

explicit_text: My grandfather built and ran the old Union Hotel in Sonoma, California after his time in San Francisco. The hotel was eventually razed and replaced by a Bank of America branch, but the cornerstone of the old building was found during that process. The Union Hotel was a significant part of his hotel business legacy outside the city.

explicit_text: My mother’s family came from southern Germany, where my other grandfather Backer was a baker. He worked in San Francisco at the Winkle bakery on Sutter Street. So Germany was the origin of my maternal family and influenced our family’s trades and lives in California.
